nodes.x: Lord, how are they increased that trouble me! many are they that rise up against me.
Cry over the flocks of your fields, that they may increase.
Many a time have they afflicted me from my youth: yet they have not prevailed against me.

But behold, there are those who do joy in your afflictions, yea, insomuch that they have risen up in rebellion against me, and also those of my people who are freemen, yea, and those who have risen up are exceedingly numerous.
I have wounded them that they were not able to rise: they are fallen under my feet.
As they were increased, so they sinned against me: therefore will I change their glory into shame.
Many there be which say of my soul, There is no help for him in God. Selah.
What man is he that liveth, and shall not see death? shall he deliver his soul from the hand of the grave? Selah.
Our soul waiteth for the Lord: he is our help and our shield.
Behold, God is mine helper: the Lord is with them that uphold my soul.
He shall send from heaven, and save me from the reproach of him that would swallow me up. Selah. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.
For strangers are risen up against me, and oppressors seek after my soul: they have not set God before them. Selah.
But thou, O Lord, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.
And that bringeth me forth from mine enemies: thou also hast lifted me up on high above them that rose up against me: thou hast delivered me from the violent man.
And now shall mine head be lifted up above mine enemies round about me: therefore will I offer in his tabernacle sacrifices of joy; I will sing, yea, I will sing praises unto the Lord.
O God the Lord, the strength of my salvation, thou hast covered my head in the day of battle.
I will extol thee, O Lord; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.
Thou art my hiding place and my shield: I hope in thy word.
I cried unto the Lord with my voice, and he heard me out of his holy hill. Selah.
In my distress I cried unto the Lord, and he heard me.
I cried unto God with my voice, even unto God with my voice; and he gave ear unto me.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried unto my God: he heard my voice out of his temple, and my cry came before him, even into his ears.
I cried unto the Lord with my voice; with my voice unto the Lord did I make my supplication.
In my distress I called upon the Lord, and cried to my God: and he did hear my voice out of his temple, and my cry did enter into his ears.
I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the Lord sustained me.
Awake, why sleepest thou, O Lord? arise, cast us not off for ever.
Then the Lord awaked as one out of sleep, and like a mighty man that shouteth by reason of wine.
Now as he was speaking with me, I was in a deep sleep on my face toward the ground: but he touched me, and set me upright.
Upon this I awaked, and beheld; and my sleep was sweet unto me.

Awake thou that sleepest, arise from the dead and Christ shall give thee light. The word of God is powerful to quicken and arouse from the lethargy of unbelief, and when men hear unto conviction of truth, let them do, and partake of the milk and wine of Christ's Gospel until they can
I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people, that have set themselves against me round about.
Behold, ye have breathed out many threatenings against me and my people; but behold, we fear not your threatenings.
Ye shall not fear them: for the Lord your God he shall fight for you.

then look forth for my coming, and know that it is nigh at hand. In relation to the world, our enemies, their soldiery, and their governors, I do not fear them, and I never did. If you will do right—keep the commandments of God, I can say with all the propriety that any man, prophet, or apostle ever did, you shall

And I looked, and rose up, and said unto the nobles, and to the rulers, and to the rest of the people, Be not ye afraid of them: remember the Lord, which is great and terrible, and fight for your brethren, your sons, and your daughters, your wives, and your houses.
Let those that fear thee turn unto me, and those that have known thy testimonies.
Arise, O Lord; save me, O my God: for thou hast smitten all mine enemies upon the cheek bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly.
The Lord shall cause thine enemies that rise up against thee to be smitten before thy face: they shall come out against thee one way, and flee before thee seven ways.

And mine eye shall not spare, neither will I have pity: I will recompense thee according to thy ways and thine abominations that are in the midst of thee; and ye shall know that I am the Lord that smiteth.
But I say unto you, that ye shall not resist evil, but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also;
Blessed be the Lord, who hath not given us as a prey to their teeth.
Salvation belongeth unto the Lord: thy blessing is upon thy people. Selah.
God be merciful unto us, and bless us; and cause his face to shine upon us; Selah.
All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thee; they shall sing to thy name. Selah.
Blessed be the Lord, who daily loadeth us with benefits, even the God of our salvation. Selah.
Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people, thou hast covered all their sin. Selah.
